dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9672 IH: Vaccine Injury Compensation Modernization Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Doggett (for himself and Mr. Smucker) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To amend the Public Health Service Act to make updates to the Vaccine Injury Compensation Program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Vaccine Injury Compensation Modernization Act of 2026.

section.header: Changes to Vaccine Injury Compensation Program

paragraph.header: Establish minimum number of special masters
paragraph.text: Section 2112(c)(1) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–12(c)(1)) is amended by striking not more than 8 and inserting not less than 10.
Section 2112(c)(4) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–12(c)(4)) is amended to read as follows:

The appointment of any individual as a special master shall be for an initial term of 4 years, subject to termination under paragraphs (2) and (3). An individual appointed as special master may be reappointed to serve one or more additional terms of up to 8 years each, pursuant to paragraph (1), and subject to termination under paragraphs (2) and (3).
Section 2112(c)(6)(E) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–12(c)(6)(E)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by inserting after disposition of petitions, the following: the number of petitions filed that are pending disposition, the number of hearings scheduled with respect to a pending disposition,; and

by inserting , including recommendations on whether additional special masters are needed to ensure an expeditious and fair resolution of petitions or otherwise improve the Program after in the Program.

subsection.text: Section 2114(e)(2) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–14(e)(2)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the matter preceding subparagraph (A)—

subparagraph.text: by striking routine administration to children and inserting administration to children, adults, or pregnant women; and
by striking within 2 years of and inserting within 6 months of; and

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (A), by striking routine administration to children and inserting administration to children, adults, or pregnant women.
Section 2115(a)(2) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–15(a)(2)) is amended to read as follows:
In the event of a vaccine-related death, an award of $600,000.
Section 2115(a)(4) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–15(a)(4)) is amended to read as follows:
For actual and projected pain and suffering and emotional distress from the vaccine-related injury, an award not to exceed $600,000.

subsection.text: Section 2116(a)(2) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–16(a)(2)) is amended by striking 36 months and inserting 5 years.

paragraph.text: Section 2111(a)(2)(A) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–11(a)(2)(A)) is amended, in the matter preceding clause (i), by inserting that includes the medical records and other information required under subsection (c) (including, if applicable, an identification of unavailable records and explanation of unavailability pursuant to subsection (c)(3)) after unless a petition.

Section 2112(d)(3)(A)(ii) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–12(d)(3)(A)(ii)) is amended by striking the petition was filed and inserting on which the petition and the medical records and other information required to be filed with such petition under section 2111(c) was filed.

subsection.text: Not later than 60 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall promulgate regulations to add, in accordance with section 2114(c)(3) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–14(c)(3)), COVID–19 vaccines to the Vaccine Injury Table. In promulgating such regulations, the Secretary shall provide for notice and opportunity for a public hearing and at least 30 days of public comment.

Notwithstanding sections 319F–3 and 319F–4 of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 247d–6d; 42 U.S.C. 247d–6e), any individual who received a COVID–19 vaccine, or any other vaccine that is added to the Vaccine Injury Table pursuant to section 2114 of such Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–14), shall be eligible to file a petition for compensation under section 2111 of such Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–11), subject to the requirements of section 2116 of such Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–16).

Section 2115(g) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–15(g)) is amended by striking the period at the end and inserting , or (3) under the Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program under sections 319F–3 and 319F–4, which shall be considered a concurrent remedy. Sections 319F–3 and 319F–4 shall not otherwise impact the availability of compensation under this Act, with respect to a vaccine-related injury or vaccine-related death.

paragraph.text: Nothing in this section, or any amendment made by this Act, shall be construed to affect or limit the application of section 319F–3 of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 247d–6d) (including the liability protections for covered countermeasures provided under such section) and any declaration made under such section 319F–3, or any amendments made to such a declaration.

Any civil action (other than a petition for compensation under the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program pursuant to section 2111 of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–11)) that is related to the administration to an individual of a COVID–19 vaccine which was, at the time of administration, a covered countermeasure, will be subject to the procedures specified in section 319F–3 of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 247d–6d), regardless of whether the individual involved has filed a petition for compensation pursuant to section 2111 of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–11) and elects, pursuant to section 2121 of such Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–21), to withdraw the petition or to file a civil action instead of accepting the compensation or judgment on the petition.

Nothing in this Act shall be construed to allow any civil action (other than a petition for compensation under the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program pursuant to section 2111 of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–11)) if, on the date of enactment of this Act, such civil action was time-barred under applicable law or that was the subject of a final judgment or order.

subsection.text: In this section, the term COVID–19 vaccine refers to any vaccine that is intended to prevent, mitigate, or limit the harm from COVID–19, or the transmission of SARS–CoV–2 or a virus mutating therefrom, including any vaccine that is licensed under section 351 of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 262) or authorized for emergency use under section 564 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 360bbb–3), regardless of the platform or technology used to produce such vaccine.
The Secretary of Health and Human Services—

paragraph.text: in consultation with the Attorney General, shall submit a budget outlining the resource needs for each agency for purposes of carrying out the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program under subtitle 2 of title XXI of such Act (42 U.S.C. 300aa–10 et seq.) for fiscal years 2027 through 2031; and

shall submit a budget outlining resource needs for purposes of carrying out the Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program under section 319F–4 of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 247d–6e) for fiscal years 2027 through 2031.

subsection.text: The budgets described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a) shall include estimates of both—

paragraph.text: the resources necessary to process current backlogs under each program referred to in such subsection; and
each program’s ability to reduce processing times for claims under the programs referred to in such paragraphs.

section.header: Addition of miscellaneous vaccines to list of taxable vaccines

paragraph.text: Section 4132(a)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: Any vaccine against dengue.
The amendment made by paragraph (1) shall apply to sales and uses on or after the later of—

clause.text: the first day of the first month which begins more than 4 weeks after the date of the enactment of this Act, or

the date on which the Secretary of Health and Human Services lists any vaccine against dengue (other than any vaccine against dengue listed by the Secretary prior to the date of the enactment of this Act) for purposes of compensation for any vaccine-related injury or death through the Vaccine Injury Compensation Trust Fund.

subparagraph.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A) and section 4131 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, in the case of sales on or before the effective date described in such subparagraph for which delivery is made after such date, the delivery date shall be considered the sale date.

paragraph.text: Section 4132(a)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended by subsection (a)(1), is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: Any vaccine against SARS–CoV–2.
The amendment made by paragraph (1) shall apply to sales and uses on or after the later of—

clause.text: the first day of the first month which begins more than 4 weeks after the date of the enactment of this Act, or

the date on which the Secretary of Health and Human Services lists any vaccine against SARS–CoV–2 (other than any vaccine against SARS–CoV–2 listed by the Secretary prior to the date of the enactment of this Act) for purposes of compensation for any vaccine-related injury or death through the Vaccine Injury Compensation Trust Fund.

Except as provided in clause (ii), for purposes of subparagraph (A) and section 4131 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, in the case of sales on or before the effective date described in such subparagraph for which delivery is made after such date, the delivery date shall be considered the sale date.
Clause (i) shall not apply to any sale to the United States Government.

paragraph.text: Section 4132(a)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended by subsection (b)(1), is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: Any vaccine against respiratory syncytial virus.
The amendment made by paragraph (1) shall apply to sales and uses on or after the later of—

clause.text: the first day of the first month which begins more than 4 weeks after the date of the enactment of this Act, or

the date on which the Secretary of Health and Human Services lists any vaccine against respiratory syncytial virus (other than any vaccine against respiratory syncytial virus listed by the Secretary prior to the date of the enactment of this Act) for purposes of compensation for any vaccine-related injury or death through the Vaccine Injury Compensation Trust Fund.

subparagraph.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A) and section 4131 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, in the case of sales on or before the effective date described in such subparagraph for which delivery is made after such date, the delivery date shall be considered the sale date.

paragraph.text: Section 4132(a)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended by subsection (c)(1), is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: Any vaccine against herpes zoster (shingles).
The amendment made by paragraph (1) shall apply to sales and uses on or after the later of—

clause.text: the first day of the first month which begins more than 4 weeks after the date of the enactment of this Act, or

the date on which the Secretary of Health and Human Services lists any vaccine against herpes zoster (shingles) (other than any vaccine against herpes zoster (shingles) listed by the Secretary prior to the date of the enactment of this Act) for purposes of compensation for any vaccine-related injury or death through the Vaccine Injury Compensation Trust Fund.

subparagraph.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A) and section 4131 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, in the case of sales on or before the effective date described in such subparagraph for which delivery is made after such date, the delivery date shall be considered the sale date.

paragraph.text: Section 4132(a)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended by subsection (d)(1), is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: Any vaccine not described in this paragraph—

subclause.text: is licensed under section 351 of the Public Health Service Act, or
is authorized for emergency use under section 564 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act, and

clause.text: which is commercially distributed in the United States.

subparagraph.text: The amendment made by paragraph (1) shall apply to sales and uses of vaccines described in subparagraph (U) of section 4132(a)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended by paragraph (1), on or after the later of—

clause.text: the first day of the first month which begins more than 4 weeks after the date of the enactment of this Act, or

the date on which the Secretary of Health and Human Services lists any such vaccine (other than any such vaccine listed by the Secretary prior to the date of the enactment of this Act) for purposes of compensation for any vaccine-related injury or death through the Vaccine Injury Compensation Trust Fund.

subparagraph.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A) and section 4131 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, in the case of sales on or before the effective date described in such subparagraph for which delivery is made after such date, the delivery date shall be considered the sale date.

paragraph.text: Section 4132(a)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by inserting by stimulating active immunity or by providing passive immunity for long-term protection through long-acting monoclonal antibody products included in recommendations of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices that have been adopted by the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention before the period.
The amendment made by paragraph (1) shall apply to sales and uses on or after the date of the enactment of this Act.
Not later than 30 days after a vaccine—

subparagraph.text: is either—

clause.text: licensed under section 351 of the Public Health Service Act, or
authorized for emergency use under section 564 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act, and

subparagraph.text: is first commercially distributed in the United States,

paragraph.continuation-text: the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall provide notice of such license or authorization to the appropriate recipients.

paragraph.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term appropriate recipients means—

subparagraph.text: the Secretary of the Treasury,
the Committees on Ways and Means and Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives, and
the Committees on Finance and Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate.

subsection.text: Section 4131(b)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by striking 75 cents and inserting $2.20.
The amendment made by this section shall apply to sales and uses on or after the first day of the first month which begins more than 6 months after the date of the enactment of this Act.
